title: idmap only works one way
body:
### Issue Description

Idmapped mounts seem to be working only one way, when touching things from the container they become nobody:nobody. I can't really describe it better, see the next point.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

podman command:

```
/usr/bin/podman run -d --name transmission --userns=host --user=977:973 --mount=type=bind,source=/mnt/data/Downloads,destination=/mnt/data/Downloads,idmap=uids=979-977-1;gids=975-973-1 --security-opt label=disable fedora:37
```

The issue:

```
[root@server2 Downloads]# touch test.txt
[root@server2 Downloads]# ls -lna test.txt
-rw-r--r--. 1 0 0 0 May 28 18:43 test.txt
[root@server2 Downloads]# chown -R 979:975 test.txt
[root@server2 Downloads]# ls -lna test.txt
-rw-r--r--. 1 979 975 0 May 28 18:43 test.txt
[root@server2 Downloads]# podman exec -it transmission /bin/ls -lna /mnt/data/Downloads/test.txt
-rw-r--r--. 1 977 973 0 May 28 16:43 /mnt/data/Downloads/test.txt
[root@server2 Downloads]# podman exec -it transmission /bin/id -u
977
[root@server2 Downloads]# podman exec -it transmission /bin/id -g
973
[root@server2 Downloads]# rm test.txt
[root@server2 Downloads]# podman exec -it transmission /bin/touch /mnt/data/Downloads/test.txt
/bin/touch: setting times of '/mnt/data/Downloads/test.txt': Permission denied
[root@server2 Downloads]# ls -lna test.txt
-rw-r--r--. 1 977 973 0 May 28 18:45 test.txt
[root@server2 Downloads]# podman exec -it transmission /bin/ls -lna /mnt/data/Downloads/test.txt
-rw-r--r--. 1 65534 65534 0 May 28 16:45 /mnt/data/Downloads/test.txt
```


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

This used to work as you'd expect

comments.body:
interestingly, rolling back to 4.4.4 didn't fix the issue
nor did rolling back the kernel to 6.2.13-300.fc38

it works well for me using your same reproducer.

What is the file ownership for the `/mnt/data/Downloads/test.txt` file on the host?  What is the underlying file system for `/mnt/data/Downloads`?

> it works well for me using your same reproducer.

Odd. It used to work for me too until about the 17th of this month, not sure which package update broke it

> What is the file ownership for the /mnt/data/Downloads/test.txt file on the host?

see `ls -lna` in the original post ran on both the internally and externally created file

it seems to keep the one passed to podman via `--user` (977:973), rather than getting mapped to 979:975

>  What is the underlying file system for /mnt/data/Downloads?

`/dev/sda1 on /var/mnt/data type ntfs3 (rw,noatime,uid=0,gid=0,iocharset=utf8,sparse,prealloc)`
I can confirm the reproducer does work as expected (and not as reported) on an ext4 filesystem

I am not familiar with `ntfs3` but that would be the first thing I'd investigate.

Can you try with a different file system?
I am closing the issue since this is a kernel bug
